articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: The Most Expensive Vote in History: Wicknell Chivayo’s Shocking $3.6 Million Offer to MPs Could Cement Mnangagwa’s Grip on Power Until 2030!
more.description: A Golden Handshake for Parliament? Chivayo’s $3.6 Million Pledge Raises Eyebrows Ahead of Crucial Vote HARARE – In a move that has ignited a fierce debate across Zimbabwe, controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo has publicly announced a staggering donation of US$3.6 million to all 360 members of parliament and senators. The flamboyant entrepreneur claims this generous [...] The post The Most Expensive Vote in History: Wicknell Chivayo’s Shocking $3.6 Million Offer to MPs Could Cement Mnangagwa’s Grip on Power Until 2030! first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: Drone scheme to speed medical supplies to remote areas
more.description: A new drone delivery initiative launched by Drone Solutions is set to transform how critical medical supplies reach remote communities in Zimbabwe, positioning the country as a growing hub for high-tech healthcare logistics in the region.

more.title: Mnangagwa pledges faster road rehabilitation, rural electrification
more.description: Source: Mnangagwa pledges faster road rehabilitation, rural electrification — CITEZW President Emmerson Mnangagwa says government is intensifying road rehabilitation and rural electrification programmes as part of efforts to modernise infrastructure and drive economic development. Addressing masses at the 46th Independence Day celebrations in Maphisa, Matabeleland South Province, Mnangagwa said the second republic is prioritising the [...] The post Mnangagwa pledges faster road rehabilitation, rural electrification appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Vendors strike gold at Independence celebrations
more.description: Source: Vendors strike gold at Independence celebrations — CITEZW Informal traders and small-scale entrepreneurs seized the opportunity presented by Zimbabwe’s 46th Independence Day celebrations in Maphisa, Matabeleland South, turning the national event into a bustling marketplace as they sold food, drinks and other goods to thousands of attendees. For the first time, the province hosted [...] The post Vendors strike gold at Independence celebrations appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Zim eyes trade boom as ZITF roars into life
more.description: Source: Zim eyes trade boom as ZITF roars into life – herald Nqobile Bhebhe Zimpapers Business Hub THE Zimbabwe International Trade Fair (ZITF) 2026 roars into life in Bulawayo tomorrow, as the high-impact trade and investment platform looks to drive Zimbabwe’s accelerated export growth, attract capital inflows and integrate into global value chains. The 66th [...] The post Zim eyes trade boom as ZITF roars into life appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: TIMB guarantees tobacco harvest will find market
more.description: Source: TIMB guarantees tobacco harvest will find market – herald Martin Kadzere THE Tobacco Industry and Marketing Board (TIMB) has provided a firm guarantee that the projected record 400 million kilogramme tobacco harvest will be fully absorbed by international off-takers, effectively neutralising market anxieties over a global supply glut. TIMB chief executive officer Mr Emmanuel [...] The post TIMB guarantees tobacco harvest will find market appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Forex inflows surge to US$4,97bn
more.description: Source: Forex inflows surge to US$4,97bn – herald Business Reporter ZIMBABWE recorded a sharp rise in foreign currency inflows in the first quarter of 2026, as strong export earnings and resilient remittances helped deliver sustained external surpluses, according to the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ). Total foreign currency receipts climbed 54,1 percent to US$4,97 billion [...] The post Forex inflows surge to US$4,97bn appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: South Africa dashes permanent residents hopes for Zimbabwean exemption permit holders
more.description: Source: South Africa dashes permanent residents hopes for Zimbabwean exemption permit holders – The Standard The ZEP programme, which began in 2009 as the Dispensation for Zimbabwe Permit, has provided a legal lifeline for thousands of Zimbabweans for over 15 years. PRETORIA – South Africa’s Department of Home Affairs (DHA) has moved to clarify the [...] The post South Africa dashes permanent residents hopes for Zimbabwean exemption permit holders appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: This is the only country we call home
more.description: Source: This is the only country we call home – herald President Mnangagwa We reproduce in full President Mnangagwa’s speech delivered on the occasion of Zimbabwe’s 46th Independence Day commemorations yesterday in Maphisa, Matabeleland South province. ********** Salutations. more.title: Zim in final push as it eyes United Nations Security Council seat
more.description: Source: Zim in final push as it eyes United Nations Security Council seat – herald Debra Matabvu PRESIDENT MNANGAGWA is expected to dispatch Foreign Affairs and International Trade Minister Professor Amon Murwira as his special envoy to Eastern Europe and other parts of Europe as Zimbabwe intensifies its diplomatic campaign for a non-permanent seat on [...] The post Zim in final push as it eyes United Nations Security Council seat appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .

more.title: Record crowd and colourful scenes mark Uhuru celebrations
more.description: Source: Record crowd and colourful scenes mark Uhuru celebrations – herald Gibson Mhaka in MAPHISA MORE than 50 000 people yesterday thronged Maphisa Stadium in Matobo district, Matabeleland South province, for Zimbabwe’s 46th main Independence Day celebrations, in a historic event that combined a bumper crowd with a vibrant display of national unity and pride. more.title: President Boko jets in for State visit
more.description: Source: President Boko jets in for State visit – herald Zimpapers Reporter BOTSWANA President Duma Boko is expected in Zimbabwe on Tuesday for a three-day State visit, where he will co-chair a high-level bilateral meeting and also officially open this year’s Zimbabwe International Trade Fair (ZITF). more.title: Consumers get relief as Government slashes fuel prices
more.description: Source: Consumers get relief as Government slashes fuel prices – herald Zimpapers Reporter CONSUMERS yesterday heaved a sigh of relief after the Government slashed fuel prices, easing pressure on households and businesses following weeks of rising costs driven by conflict in the Middle East. The Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority (ZERA) announced on Friday that the [...] The post Consumers get relief as Government slashes fuel prices appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation .
